sglang.title: Add a new arguments log_level_http to control the HTTP logging
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt: Add --log-level-http warning to disable most info logging from the unicorn http server.

sglang.title: Add sglang.bench_latency for offline benchmark
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Add sglang/bench_latency.py. It is a very convenient utility for offline test.

Usage (latency test):
>>> python -m sglang.bench_latency --model-path meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B-Instruct --load-format dummy --tp 2

Prefill. latency:  5.021 ms, throughput:    203.93 token/s
Decode.  latency:  0.331 ms

sglang.title: Warmup cublas
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt: We need to run a small matmul to init cublas. Otherwise, it will raise some errors later.

vllm.title: [Doc] Add notice about breaking changes to VLMs
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: After some offline discussion with @ywang96 and @xwjiang2010, we have planned a few breaking changes to VLMs. This PR updates the docs to notify users about this in advance.

vllm.title: [Speculative Decoding] Support draft model on different tensor-parallel size than target model
vllm.author: wooyeonlee0
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR supports multi-step draft workers to work with a tp degree smaller than target workers.
Note: this PR is limited to support draft_tp=1 for now, and will be extended with the following PR.
What I've done in this PR:

Added SmallerTPProposerWorker that wraps MultiStepWorker to work with a tp t

vllm.title: [Misc] Remove useless code in cpu_worker
vllm.author: DamonFool
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Hi all,
Please review this one-line change which removes useless code in cpu_worker.
Thanks.
Best regards,
Jie

vllm.title: [Core] Add fault tolerance for `RayTokenizerGroupPool`
vllm.author: Yard1
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds basic fault tolerance to RayTokenizerGroupPool, allowing it to recover from tokenizer actors deaths, and marking itself (and by extension, the engine) as unhealthy if that's not possible.



 PR Checklist (Click to Expand) 
Thank you for your contribution to vLLM! Before submitting the

vllm.title: [doc][distributed] add both gloo and nccl tests
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt: lesson learned from #5779 . we also need to test gloo communication, and set GLOO_SOCKET_IFNAME/NCCL_SOCKET_IFNAME accordingly.

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Add unit testing for FlexibleArgumentParser
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Requires #5795 for initial bugfix regarding replacement in actual arguments.

vllm.title: [Misc] Update `w4a16` `compressed-tensors` support to include `w8a16`
vllm.author: dsikka
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Expand support such that we can run w8a16 models compressed through compressed-tensors
This requires changing the supported scheme from CompressedTensorsW4A16 to CompressedTensorsWNA16 to support both w4a16 and w8a16/allow use of the same kernels

By Neural Magic

vllm.title: [Hardware][TPU] Refactor TPU backend
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR refactors the TPU backend so that its architecture is more similar to the GPU backend.

vllm.title: [Hardware][AMD][CI/Build][Doc] Upgrade to ROCm 6.1, Dockerfile improvements, test fixes
vllm.author: mawong-amd
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR does the following:

Upgrade the default base image to ROCm 6.1.2 with the correct fixes applied, and the default PyTorch version to 2.4.0, bringing it in line with CUDA's update to 2.3.0.
Refactor to enable multi-stage builds in Dockerfile.rocm: flash-attn and Triton are now built in separa

vllm.title: [Hardware][TPU] Raise errors for unsupported sampling params
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR adds errors raised when unsupported sampling params are used.

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Add E2E tests for MLPSpeculator
vllm.author: tdoublep
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds some E2E tests for MLPSpeculator (as-per discussion in #4947). They are based on the ngram speculator tests.
I have verified these tests pass using following parameters:
MAIN_MODEL = "ibm-granite/granite-3b-code-instruct"
SPEC_MODEL = "ibm-granite/granite-3b-code-instruct-accelerator"
M

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix assertion in NeuronExecutor
vllm.author: aws-patlange
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Running the examples/offline_inference_neuron.py script with vllm past runs into an assertion error in the NeuronExecutor. This is because scheduler outputs such as blocks_to_swap_in etc are empty lists but the assertion checks for empty dictionaries.
It's fixed by checking that the scheduler output

vllm.title: [Core] Refactor Worker and ModelRunner to consolidate control plane communication
vllm.author: stephanie-wang
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Currently the ModelRunner class contains both model execution code and multi-GPU control plane communication code, i.e.broadcast_tensor_dict calls. This makes it difficult to swap out the control plane mechanism, e.g., using NCCL vs CPU-based serialization to move the inputs from the LLMEngine to th

vllm.title: [Misc][Doc] Add Example of using OpenAI Server with VLM
vllm.author: ywang96
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
As a follow-up on the discussion in #5693, this PR adds an example to serve a vision language model with vLLM OpenAI API server and to call this server with OpenAI client.
In addition, this PR adds a URL validation to accept only http and https, inspired by #5771



 PR Checklist (Click to Expand)

vllm.title: [bugfix][distributed] fix shm broadcast when the queue size is full
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The shm broadcast is meant to be a message queue, and current_idx points to the enqueue / dequeue position for writer/reader. We cannot go on to find another block when the current block is not ready for write/read.
This PR fixes the bug, and also borrow the stress test from #5768 .
somehow magicall
